items.title: Every engineering claim needs provenance
items.summary: A retrieved rule or calculation must identify its source, revision, units, applicability conditions, authority, and review state before the agent presents it as engineering guidance.
items.agent_guidance: Do not convert a retrieved guideline into a compliance claim. State assumptions and source class in the answer.

items.title: Datum reference frames establish inspection orientation
items.summary: Datum features establish the physical references used to orient and locate a part for manufacturing and inspection. A primary, secondary, and tertiary scheme may constrain the six degrees of freedom required by the design intent.
items.agent_guidance: Explain datum intent, but do not approve a datum scheme or claim ASME compliance without the licensed standard and engineering review.

items.title: Feature control frames communicate geometric controls
items.summary: A feature control frame associates a geometric characteristic, tolerance, material modifier when applicable, and datum references with a controlled feature. Controls include form, orientation, location, profile, and runout families.
items.agent_guidance: Use this for terminology and retrieval routing. Escalate material-condition interpretation, virtual condition, and compliance questions to the licensed ASME reference and a qualified reviewer.

items.title: Bend allowance and bend deduction
items.summary: For a single bend, bend allowance is computed from inside bend radius, material thickness, K-factor, and bend angle. Bend deduction converts outside flange dimensions to a flat-pattern length.
conditions: Use a K-factor validated for the specified material, thickness, tooling, and forming process.
Angles are in degrees and lengths are in millimetres.
Confirm dimensions and flat pattern with the fabricator before release.
items.agent_guidance: Never invent a K-factor. Ask for it or identify the result as a preliminary estimate.

items.title: Bend-relief screening
items.summary: When adjacent unbent material would tear, warp, or interfere during a bend, a relief feature may be needed. The supplied guide gives a minimum-width heuristic and a depth heuristic based on material thickness and bend radius.
conditions: The 0.508 mm clearance originates from a 0.020 inch guideline.
Relief geometry depends on tooling and part function.
This is a DFM screen, not a released manufacturing requirement.
items.agent_guidance: Report passing checks as preliminary only and request supplier capability confirmation.

items.title: Hole and slot screening
items.summary: The supplied guide recommends a hole or slot width no smaller than material thickness and a hole-to-hole spacing of at least twice material thickness. It also contains conflicting hole-to-edge guidance, so edge-distance checks remain review-only.
conditions: The source text and diagram disagree on hole-to-edge distance; do not make an automatic pass/fail decision for that dimension.
Minimum feature size depends on process, material, thickness, and supplier capability.
items.agent_guidance: Use the numeric checks as warnings. For edge distance, explicitly request review rather than guessing a threshold.

items.title: Grain direction affects bend risk
items.summary: For materials with a directional grain, bends parallel to the grain can be more susceptible to cracking than bends perpendicular to it. Some processes cannot guarantee grain orientation, so this must be a supplier capability input.
items.agent_guidance: Flag grain direction as an unresolved supplier constraint when bend quality or cracking risk is important.

items.title: Thread and hole dimensions require series-specific sources
items.summary: Metric and Unified thread callouts require a complete series, nominal size, pitch or threads-per-inch, fit, engagement assumptions, and the applicable drill or clearance class. A chart can seed retrieval, but released dimensions must be tied to the selected standard and tooling source.
items.agent_guidance: Use the existing fastener lookup for its scoped values. Do not infer a tap drill for a fine or Unified thread until the new catalogue records its exact source and revision.
